task_completion.description: Verify the agent created a 3-generation genogram in draw.io with all 7 required individuals labeled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the draw.io application open with a family tree structure containing three distinct vertical levels (Generations I, II, III). The shapes must be labeled with the names: Arthur, Betty, Charles, Diana, Edward, Alice, and Frank.

task_completion.description: Verify correct shapes are used for males and females.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Males (Arthur, Charles, Edward, Frank) must be represented by squares or rectangles. Females (Betty, Diana, Alice) must be represented by circles or ellipses.

task_completion.description: Verify correct fill colors are used to indicate Huntington's Disease status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Affected individuals (Arthur, Charles) must have a solid fill (e.g., black or a dark color). Unaffected individuals (Betty, Diana, Edward, Alice, Frank) must have a white or transparent (no fill) background.

task_completion.description: Verify deceased individuals are marked correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Deceased individuals (Arthur, Betty) must have a diagonal line or an 'X' drawn through their shape. The living individuals (Charles, Diana, Edward, Alice, Frank) must not have any strike-through lines.

task_completion.description: Verify the hierarchical and partnership connections are drawn correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Partners (Arthur-Betty, Charles-Diana) must be connected by horizontal lines. Children must drop down from their parents' partnership line: Charles and Edward drop from Arthur/Betty; Alice and Frank drop from Charles/Diana.

task_completion.description: Verify the proband (Alice) is marked with a question mark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The shape representing Alice must contain a '?' symbol, either inside the shape or placed immediately next to it, indicating unknown genetic status.

task_completion.description: Verify the genogram was saved and exported with the correct filenames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'genogram.drawio' and 'genogram.png' on the Desktop, or look for save/export dialogs in draw.io showing these exact filenames being saved to the Desktop.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the draw.io graphical interface to build the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io desktop app open with shapes being actively placed, connected, and styled on the canvas, rather than the agent writing XML code in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the exported PNG matches the work done in draw.io.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final exported image or the image seen on the desktop should visually match the diagram constructed in the draw.io canvas.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply download a pre-made genogram.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots should show the step-by-step construction of the genogram using draw.io's native shape libraries, not an imported static image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The genogram must contain exactly 7 individuals across 3 generations. Males (Squares): Arthur, Charles, Edward, Frank. Females (Circles): Betty, Diana, Alice. Affected (Solid Fill): Arthur, Charles. Unaffected (White/No Fill): Betty, Diana, Edward, Alice, Frank. Deceased (Diagonal line or 'X'): Arthur, Betty. Proband (Marked with '?'): Alice. Connections: Gen I is Arthur & Betty (partners). Gen II is Charles & Edward (children of Arthur/Betty), and Diana (partner to Charles). Gen III is Alice & Frank (children of Charles/Diana).
